CheckTitle: AI Agent Object Storage Access Must Be Logged
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Data Access**. Verifies that all object storage operations (GET, PUT, DELETE) performed by the agent are captured in access logs along with the agent identity. Object storage is a common exfiltration target and must be fully auditable.
Risk: Without object storage access logs, data exfiltration via bulk GET or covert PUT operations cannot be detected or investigated after the fact, making forensic analysis impossible.

Code.Other:
1. Enable server access logging on all S3 buckets / Azure Blob containers / GCS buckets used by the agent.
2. Ensure logs include requester identity, operation type, object key, and timestamp.
3. Forward logs to a centralised SIEM and configure alerts for unusual access patterns.

Recommendation.Text: Enable access logging on all object storage resources used by the agent. Include requester identity in logs and set up anomaly alerts for bulk downloads.

Notes: ASPM-053 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
